summary: Excellent spring‑85 show with smoking Althea and Midnight Hour, captured on a solid soundboard despite occasional muddiness.
review: The 4/8/85 Philadelphia performance is widely praised for its high energy first set – highlights include a scorching Althea, a fiery Midnight Hour/Big Boss Man medley and a crowd‑pleasing Revolution.  The recording is generally regarded as a good soundboard source, crisp enough to hear Jerry’s inventive solos (even his Sly Stone quote) though a few listeners note occasional mud.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are very positive (5‑star) and highlight strong musical moments; recording quality is mostly praised with only minor complaints, giving a combined rating in the good‑to‑great range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most reviewers describe the sound as clear and sweet; a few note it can be muddy or more “AUD‑like” than typical SBD, but overall the board is considered listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is consistently described as “on” and almost a second‑set in intensity; the second set receives mixed feedback – some love the Revolution, others feel it drifts into sloppiness.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar work gets high marks (smoky solos, Sly Stone quote) though his voice was a bit rough early ’85 and he flubbed a lyric here and there.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh is noted as “minimal” in one review; otherwise not specifically mentioned.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s vocals are described as deep and smoky between sets; keyboards get no direct critique.
band_member_comments.Drums: A brief ‘battle’ is cited between Bobby and Jerry during the GDTRFB outro, with some listeners noting a strong drum tempo.
